name: armorcodex
description: ArmorIQ intent-based security enforcement for Codex: Bash command guardrails with intent verification, optional CSRG cryptographic proofs, and audit logging. Codex hooks currently fire for Bash only — non-Bash tool calls are not directly gated. See CODEX_HARNESS_LIMITATIONS.md.

interface.shortDescription: Intent and policy enforcement for Codex Bash actions.
interface.longDescription: ArmorIQ intent-based security enforcement for OpenAI Codex. Treat as a strong Bash guardrail and audit layer, not a complete boundary for every Codex capability — Codex hooks currently emit Bash only. ArmorCodex provides plan registration through MCP, Bash command matching against the registered plan, Bash permission gating, and Bash post-run audit. Non-Bash activity (file edits, MCP tool calls, web search, app connectors) is not directly intercepted today and may need supplemental controls.
defaultPrompt: Use ArmorCodex to register an intent plan before running shell commands.
Use ArmorCodex policy tools to list or update allowed Codex command policy.

api_key.title: ArmorIQ API Key
api_key.description: Your ArmorIQ API key (get one at https://armoriq.ai). Leave blank to run in local-only mode without backend audit/intent.

mode.title: Enforcement Mode
mode.description: enforce = block on policy/intent failures (recommended). monitor = log only, never block.

intent_required.title: Require Intent Plan
intent_required.description: When true, every Bash command must be backed by a registered intent plan (Codex hooks currently only intercept Bash). Disable for advisory-only use.

crypto_policy_enabled.title: Enable Crypto Policy Binding
crypto_policy_enabled.description: Bind policy rules to a Merkle tree so post-issuance tampering is detected.

use_production.title: Use Production Endpoints
use_production.description: When true, talks to ArmorIQ production. When false, expects a local backend on 127.0.0.1.
